lines.speaker: Librarian
lines.text: Welcome to the Keldagrim library, human traveller!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can you tell me more about the library?

lines.speaker: Librarian
lines.text: Why, certainly, it is one of my favourite subjects!

lines.speaker: Librarian
lines.text: I am Hugi, you see, I am the chief librarian here. Hugi, my parents named me that, it means 'personification of thought' in the ancient dwarvish tongue.

lines.speaker: Librarian
lines.text: Quite like the name myself.

lines.speaker: Librarian
lines.text: The books in this library have been collected for many centuries now, through various generous donations.

lines.speaker: Librarian
lines.text: You'll find books dating from many different eras as well, such as the Era of Kings, the Rise of the Consortium and the Era of Prosperity, our current era.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How do these eras correspond with the Ages of the world?

lines.speaker: Librarian
lines.text: Oh, they are quite different, and most of them take place in the Fourth Age.

lines.speaker: Librarian
lines.text: Since we dwarves hid underground for a long time to sit out the wars that raged above the ground our history books differ slightly from those written by humans.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks for the information!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do you know about impenetrable rocks?

lines.speaker: Librarian
lines.text: You're confusing me, what rock exactly are you talking about?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The big one in the mines south of the city. Dondakan's big rock.

lines.speaker: Librarian
lines.text: I see, I see.

lines.speaker: Librarian
lines.text: I heard there was a demon inside there that you banished.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, what exactly does that mean anyway?

lines.speaker: Librarian
lines.text: There are some demons that we cannot see properly as mortals. They also cannot be fought with normal weapons.

lines.speaker: Librarian
lines.text: But sometimes these creatures manifest themselves in a solid form in our world. Their avatar.

lines.speaker: Librarian
lines.text: When you defeat an avatar, you defeat the actual demon behind it. But they don't die, they are banished to where they came from originally.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Does that mean I will have to deal with this demon again some time?

lines.speaker: Librarian
lines.text: Doubtful. He will grow stronger and stronger over time, but that's a process that will take a thousand years at least.

lines.speaker: Librarian
lines.text: Even dwarves don't live so long.

lines.speaker: Librarian
lines.text: No, we won't see him again and the mines are safe to dig for gold now. Of course, that might lead to other problems eventually...
